title: Kibatalia elastica
intro: Kibatalia elastica, also known as the Kibatalia tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ebenaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Kibatalia elastica include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to have potential in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Kibatalia elastica include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Kibatalia elastica is classified in the family Sapotaceae and belongs to the order Ericales. This classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants, known as angiosperms. The genus Kibatalia is part of the family Sapotaceae, which includes over 100 species. Kibatalia elastica is a tropical tree native to the Philippines, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
